name: Lat Pulldown (Wide Grip)
description: Sit at the lat pulldown station. Grip the bar wider than shoulder width, overhand grip. Pull the bar down to upper chest while squeezing shoulder blades together. Control the return (3 sec negative).
purpose: Builds toward pull-ups. Primary lat and upper back development.
cues: Pull elbows toward hips, not behind you

name: Assisted Pull-up (Band/Machine)
equipment: Pull-up bar + resistance band OR assisted machine
description: Loop a resistance band over the pull-up bar and place one knee/foot in the loop. Grip the bar overhand, wider than shoulders. Pull your chin above the bar, control the descent.
purpose: Real pull-up movement pattern with reduced bodyweight load. Direct progression toward unassisted pull-ups.

name: Pull-up (Unassisted)
description: Dead hang from pull-up bar, overhand grip slightly wider than shoulders. Pull chin above bar. Control descent to full dead hang.
purpose: TARGET EXERCISE — 5 unbroken by Week 20.

name: Machine Chest Press
description: Sit in machine, grip handles at chest height. Press forward to full arm extension. Control the return.
purpose: Builds chest pressing strength safely. Foundation for push-ups.

name: Incline Push-up (Hands on Bench)
description: Place hands on the edge of a bench, shoulder width apart. Body straight from head to heels. Lower chest toward bench, push back up.
purpose: Transition from machine to bodyweight. Reduced load push-up.

name: Push-up (Full / From Knees)
description: Standard push-up position. Hands slightly wider than shoulders, body straight. Lower chest to floor (or 2cm from floor), push back up. If unable to do full push-ups, start from knees.
purpose: TARGET EXERCISE — 30 unbroken by Week 20.
cues: Do max full reps, then drop to knees for remaining

name: Leg Press
description: Sit in leg press, feet shoulder-width on platform. Lower the weight with control until knees reach 90°. Press through heels to full extension.
purpose: Safe quad and glute development. Protects lower back (unlike squats initially).

name: Goblet Squat
description: Hold a kettlebell at chest height with both hands. Squat down with chest up, knees tracking over toes, to below parallel. Stand back up.
purpose: Free-weight squat pattern with natural counterbalance. Builds toward barbell squat.
cues: Chest up, elbows between knees at bottom

name: Barbell Back Squat (Light)
description: Bar on upper back (high bar position). Feet shoulder width, toes slightly out. Squat to parallel or below. Stand up.
purpose: King of lower body exercises. Use belt for lower back support.
cues: Belt recommended for lower back protection

name: Front Squat (Barbell)
description: Bar rests on front deltoids, crossed-arm or clean grip. Squat to parallel or below. Forces upright torso.
purpose: Upright torso protects lower back. Quad-dominant. Builds toward burpee squats.
cues: If wrists hurt, use crossed-arm grip

name: Squat Jump (Bodyweight)
description: Bodyweight squat to full depth, then explode upward into a jump. Land softly, immediately descend into next squat.
purpose: Explosive power. Directly prepares for burpee + squat target.
cues: Land softly on balls of feet

name: Machine Shoulder Press
description: Sit with back supported. Press handles overhead to full extension. Control the descent.
purpose: Safe shoulder pressing with back support.

name: Dumbbell Shoulder Press (Seated)
description: Sit on bench set to ~85°. Press dumbbells from shoulder height to overhead lockout.
purpose: Free weight pressing. Greater stabiliser engagement than machine.

name: Standing Barbell Overhead Press
description: Stand with bar at collarbone height. Press overhead to lockout. No leg drive (strict press).
purpose: Full-body stability under overhead load. Excellent core engagement.
cues: Bar path is slightly backward (around face)

name: Dead Bug
description: Lie on back, arms pointing to ceiling, knees bent 90°. Extend opposite arm and leg toward floor while keeping lower back pressed into mat. Return and switch sides.
purpose: Safest core exercise for people with lower back issues. Teaches core bracing.
cues: Lower back MUST stay flat on the floor
If lower back lifts, reduce range of motion

name: Plank (Knees or Full)
description: Forearms on ground, body straight from head to heels (or knees for modification). Hold position. Squeeze glutes and brace core.
purpose: TARGET EXERCISE — 5 minutes continuous by Week 20.
cues: Hips level — don't sag or pike

name: Machine Bicep Curl
description: Sit at machine. Curl handles toward shoulders with control. Slow negative.
purpose: Isolated bicep development. Builds foundation for chin-ups.

name: Dumbbell Bicep Curl (Standing)
description: Stand with dumbbells at sides. Curl both or alternate to shoulder height. Slow negative.
purpose: Free weight bicep work with standing core engagement.

name: Assisted Chin-up (Supinated Grip)
description: Same setup as assisted pull-up but with underhand (supinated) grip, hands shoulder-width. Pull chin above bar.
purpose: Direct chin-up practice with assistance. Targets biceps more than overhand grip.

name: Chin-up (Unassisted)
description: Dead hang, underhand grip, shoulder width. Pull chin above bar. Control descent to full dead hang.
purpose: TARGET EXERCISE — 8 unbroken by Week 20.

name: Bench Dip (Feet on Floor)
description: Hands on bench behind you, fingers forward. Feet on floor. Lower body by bending elbows until upper arms are parallel to floor. Press back up.
purpose: Introduction to dip pattern at reduced bodyweight.

name: Dip (Assisted with Band)
equipment: Dip bars + resistance band (FSC cross-training zone)
description: Loop band under knees. Grip parallel dip bars, arms straight. Lower body until upper arms parallel to floor. Press back up.
purpose: Full dip movement pattern with band assistance. Progression toward unassisted dips.
cues: Slight forward lean for chest emphasis

name: Dip (Unassisted)
description: Grip parallel dip bars. Arms straight at top. Lower until upper arms are parallel to floor (or slightly below). Press back up to lockout.
purpose: TARGET EXERCISE — 15 unbroken by Week 20.

name: Burpee (Modified)
description: Step back to plank (don't jump), lower chest to floor or skip push-up, step forward, stand up. No squat or jump yet.
purpose: Introduction to burpee movement pattern at manageable intensity.

name: Burpee (Full)
description: Squat down, jump feet back to plank, push-up (from knees if needed), jump feet forward to squat position, stand.
purpose: Full burpee without the added squat. Builds cardiovascular endurance.

name: Burpee + Added Squat
description: Full burpee: squat down, jump back to plank, push-up, jump feet forward, DEEP SQUAT (hold for 1 second), then stand. The added squat makes each rep significantly harder.
purpose: TARGET EXERCISE — 50 unbroken by Week 20.
cues: Pace yourself — this is a 5-8 minute effort at 50 reps
Breathe: exhale on exertion, inhale on recovery

name: Bulgarian Split Squat
description: Rear foot elevated on bench behind you. Front foot forward. Lunge down until rear knee nearly touches floor. Press up through front heel.
purpose: Single-leg strength and balance. Addresses left-right imbalances.

name: Treadmill Walk (Incline)
description: Walk on treadmill at moderate pace with incline. Used after every circuit session and on active recovery days.
purpose: Low-impact cardio that burns calories without stressing joints. Incline increases intensity without impact.
